About Me

Strategy-first.
Story-led.

I'm a marketing graduate with an MSc in Business with Marketing from the University of Warwick, where I built a rigorous foundation in brand strategy, consumer behaviour, and digital engagement. My real education has been in execution — building campaigns from scratch, directing content, and measuring what actually moves an audience.

At House of Sinha, I planned and produced end-to-end integrated marketing campaigns across two major events — the Desi Rang Rave and the Valentine's Party — bringing over 300 people through the door using Instagram Reels, WhatsApp broadcasts, email, and on-ground promotion.

I bridge the gap between strategic rigour and creative instinct — which is exactly what modern marketing demands.

◈

Strategic Thinking

Every content piece exists for a reason — reach, save, trust, or conversion.

◉

Content Execution

10+ Reels produced across two live events, directed and optimised for performance.

◎

Campaign Planning

Multi-channel campaigns with structured timelines, KPIs, and real outcomes.

◌

Consumer Insight

Case Study 01 Nov 2025 — Feb 2026 2 Live Events

House of Sinha —
Two Campaigns, One Strategy

End-to-end integrated marketing across two distinct event campaigns — the high-energy Desi Rang Rave and the romantic Valentine's Party — each requiring its own creative identity, audience approach, and channel strategy, while sitting under a unified brand voice.

Role
Marketing & Content Strategist
Scope
Content Strategy · Production · Campaign Execution
Channels
Instagram · WhatsApp · Email · On-ground
Outcome
300+ Attendees · 10+ Reels · 10+ Locations
Desi Rang Rave House of Sinha × Par-Desi Party UK Kasbah, Coventry · Dec 11
Strategic Insight

The brief: promote a Bollywood × South Indian rave collaboration at Kasbah, Coventry to South Asian students across UK universities, in a crowded pre-Christmas event calendar. The challenge was cutting through end-of-term noise while speaking directly to a culturally dual audience who wanted both familiarity and spectacle.

Research revealed that cinematic Reels with bold colour, trending audio, and cultural signalling consistently outperformed generic student event content. On-campus physicality — ticket tables, flyer distribution, visible piazza presence — drove the trust signal that converted digital interest into ticket purchases.

Audience

South Asian diaspora students at UK universities. Respond to cultural pride, Bollywood energy, and community FOMO.

Platform

Instagram Reels as the primary reach engine. WhatsApp for direct conversion. Stories for real-time urgency.

Content Arc

Desire → Social Proof → Urgency. Three-phase narrative ending in sold-out messaging.

Creative Direction

The event poster set the visual world: psychedelic multicolour, neon-adjacent, raw rave energy. The task was carrying that identity across every Reel in a way that felt native to Instagram rather than a reposted flyer. Lighting, colour grading, and editing rhythm were matched to the poster’s visual language — warm saturation, kinetic movement, bold contrast — creating coherence across every touchpoint.

Campaign Photos
On-campus story · Warwick Arts Centre
Ticket booth · Kitchen Courtyard · "Few tickets left"
Event night · Desi Rang Rave · Kasbah

Valentine’s Party House of Sinha Feb 2026
Strategic Insight

The Valentine’s campaign required a fundamentally different strategic approach. Where Desi Rang Rave was built on collective spectacle, this event needed to sell intimacy, romance, and personal aspiration. The same South Asian student audience demanded a different emotional trigger — shifting from community hype to individual desire.

The content strategy pivoted accordingly: warmer tones, softer editing, storytelling centred on couple moments, getting dressed up, and the feeling of a special night out. The visual palette moved from neon-saturated to rose gold and deep red, precisely matching the emotional register of the occasion.

Emotional Hook

Romance, aspiration, the desire to celebrate. Content spoke to couples and individuals alike — inclusively and aspirationally.

Visual Shift

From rave-neon to rose gold and velvet red. Slower, warmer edits matched the romantic register of the event.

Urgency Mechanism

Valentine’s Day is a hard deadline — date proximity created natural urgency without manufactured scarcity tactics.

Creative Direction

The Valentine’s content identity was built around the idea of a curated night out — glamorous, intimate, and culturally resonant. Reels featured outfit styling, venue atmosphere, and aspirational moments set to romantic trending audio. On-ground activation was timed to the week before Valentine’s Day to intercept decision-stage behaviour.

🔥
Signature Piece

The Reverse Fire Reel

The campaign’s most distinctive creative moment — a reverse fire video edited to sync with the track drop, with lighting and colour grade matched to the Valentine’s event poster. Visually arresting, deeply scroll-stopping, and perfectly aligned with the romantic tension of a Valentine’s night out. The coherence between Reel and poster made every touchpoint feel part of the same intentional world.

Instagram

Reels for aspirational reach, Stories for countdown and ticket urgency. Rose gold and deep red palette throughout.

WhatsApp Broadcast

Direct messaging to existing community — higher personal resonance for a Valentine’s night-out event.

✉️

Email Marketing

Valentine’s-themed drip sequence: event announcement, ticket CTA, and last-minute reminder.

📍

On-Ground Activation

Campus flyer runs timed to the week before Valentine’s Day to intercept students at the decision-making stage.

Integrated Channel Strategy — Desi Rang Rave
📱

Instagram

Primary reach engine. Reels for new audiences, Stories for urgency, Feed for visual brand identity matching the event poster.

💬

WhatsApp Broadcast

Highest-conversion channel. Used at the bottom of the funnel for direct ticket push and sold-out urgency messaging.

✉️

Email Marketing

Structured drip communications for event awareness and ticket reminders, targeting registered interest segments.

📍

On-Ground Activation

Physical presence at 10+ university locations — ticket tables, flyer runs, and campus piazza distribution at Warwick and partner universities.

Combined Campaign Results — House of Sinha
300+Total Event Attendees
10+Reels Produced & Published
10+University Locations Reached
2Full Event Campaigns Owned
Key Learnings
  • 01The same audience needs a different strategy for each event. The Desi Rang Rave and Valentine's campaigns shared a target audience but required entirely different emotional registers, visual palettes, and content arcs. Recognising this — and executing accordingly — was the most important strategic decision of the role.
  • 02Integrated channels amplify each other. Reels built awareness, WhatsApp converted, on-ground created trust. Each channel played a specific role in the funnel rather than duplicating effort.
  • 03Visual coherence is a strategic tool. Matching Reel aesthetics to each event's poster created a recognisable brand world that made every piece of content feel intentional and build cumulative impact.
  • 04Urgency must be earned, not manufactured. Scarcity messaging worked because earlier content had already built genuine desire. Without that foundation, "few tickets left" is noise.
Case Study 02 MSc · Marketing Through Social Media · Warwick 2025

Fenty Beauty vs. Rare Beauty —
Visibility Doesn't Equal Engagement

A data-driven comparative analysis of two celebrity-founded beauty brands — examining why a smaller audience can systematically outperform a larger one through intentional community-building.

Methodology
50+ Content Pieces Analysed
Platforms
Instagram · TikTok · Facebook
Tools Used
SEMrush · HypeAuditor · Statista
Frameworks Applied
AIDA Model · Engagement Theory

Rare Beauty achieved 4.13 million engagements versus Fenty's 1.5 million — with less than half the follower count. Community-led content, when anchored in authentic mission, consistently outperforms visibility-first strategies.

— SEMrush Data, Feb–Mar 2025 · Analysis by Khushi Gupta
Strategic Comparison
DimensionFenty BeautyRare Beauty
Brand PositioningHigh-fashion empowerment, bold inclusivity, cultural statementEmotional wellness, self-acceptance, authentic community
Content StyleEditorial, influencer-led, trend-driven, Rihanna-anchoredSoft tutorials, UGC, mental health storytelling, pastel tones
Instagram Followers13.2 Million8.15 Million
Instagram Engagements (Mar 2025)1.81M (76 posts)5.27M (66 posts) ↑
Follower Growth (Mar 2025)+20,351+94,323 ↑
Key Campaign#TheNextFentyFace · Savage x Fenty Show BTS#WeAreRare · #RareRoutine · Rare Impact Fund
Theory AlignmentAIDA — Attention, social proof, FOMO-driven desireSocial Media Engagement Theory — community, trust, belonging
01

Authenticity Outperforms Authority

Rare Beauty's emotion-first content generated more engagement per post than Fenty's editorial output — proving relatability drives deeper audience behaviour than prestige.

02

Purpose Creates Loyalty

The Rare Impact Fund (targeting $100M for mental health) transformed product-purchase into social participation — community rather than consumers.

03

Metrics Must Match Objectives

Fenty optimises for visibility; Rare for engagement. Measuring them by the same KPI produces misleading conclusions about effectiveness.

Media Reference
SEMrush Report · Fenty Beauty · Mar 2025
SEMrush Report · Rare Beauty · Mar 2025
Mock Project Social Media Strategy Concept Design

Lea Clothing —
1-Week Instagram Strategy

A fully developed Instagram content strategy for a heritage Indian fashion brand — with every post format chosen for a specific strategic reason and a clear rationale connecting creative decisions to business outcomes.

Objective
Design a 7-Day Instagram Content Plan
Brand Positioning
Heritage + Modern Luxury · Festive Storytelling
Target Audience
Gen Z + Premium Millennial Women
Format Range
Reels · Carousels · Static · BTS
Content Pillars & Strategic Logic
Colour Storytelling Product Styling Reels → Reach BTS → Trust Sale → Conversion Emotional Storytelling

Each format serves a distinct funnel role: Reels drive discovery; Carousels generate saves and repeat views; BTS builds intimacy and brand trust; Sale content creates the conversion moment when desire is already established. No post exists without purpose.

7-Day Content Calendar
Monday
Carousel · 11:00 AM
The Nazneen Edit: Hues of Heritage
Festive palette — gold, sage, navy, champagne. Heritage moodboard, antique textures.
Carousel · 6:30 PM
The Festive Palette: Colour That Speaks Couture
Ivory, ruby, butter, amethyst — each hue in its own cinematic moment.
Tuesday
Reel · 6:45 PM
Depth & Drapes: Shadowed Glamour
Cinematic half-lit portrait. Velvet drapes, slow camera pan, amber grade.
Reel · 8:00 PM
Steal the Glow: Festive Fits on the Go
Sale announcement. Playful, luminous. Up to 40% OFF — festive urgency frame.
Wednesday
Static · 7:00 PM
Nazneen Series: Reflect in Gold
Sayra Gold Mirror Saree. Sunset light, warm amber grade, Playfair overlay.
Reel · 6:30 PM
Shake & Shine: The Rose Gold Rhyme
Beat-sync Reel — rose gold embroidered lehenga, shimmer on drop.
Thursday
Carousel · 6:00 PM
One Blouse, Many Wows
Aarvi Mirror Blouse styled 3 ways. Drive saves + styling inspiration.
Reel BTS · 8:00 PM
Glow Behind the Show: Lea BTS Diaries
Candid shoot moments. Authenticity and human touch. Trust-building post.
Friday
Reel · 6:45 PM
A Radiant Affair: The Mirror Muse
Ayinaat Mirror Dupatta. Slow motion, golden-hour glow. Premium product focus.
Reel · 6:45 PM
Glow for Less: The Festive Steal Edit
Weekend sale push. Gen Z sparkle. Pink-gold backlight. High engagement play.
Saturday
Reel · 6:00 PM
Moonlit Melodies + Mirror Glam
3 looks, 1 night. Champagne → navy → blush. Beat-drop transitions.
Static · 8:00 PM
Lightly Luxe: Everyday Glam, The Saanjh Way
Soft, airy, effortless. Introduces Saanjh Light collection identity.
Sunday
Static · 7:00 PM
Ease in Elegance: The Saanjh Story
Emotional closure post. Quiet, graceful. Morning light. Brand storytelling over product selling.
Sample Caption — Depth & Drapes
Tuesday · 6:45 PM · Reel

"In shadows and silk, the light chooses her. Draped in pearls, she becomes the depth itself. #DepthAndDrape #LeaMood #VelvetGlamour #FestiveMagic #LeaClothingCo"

Sample Caption — The Nazneen Edit
Monday · 11:00 AM · Carousel

"Your festive palette, redefined. From Champagne whispers to Sage calm, every hue in our Nazneen Edit carries a story — stitched in heritage, woven in light. #NazneenByLea #FestiveMoodboard #HeritageInHue #LeaGlow"

Instructions — How to Add Your Videos

To embed your MP4 Reels directly into this portfolio, open the HTML file in any text editor (Notepad, VS Code, etc). Find a <div class="media-video"> block you want to replace. Delete the entire block and replace it with:

<video style="width:100%;aspect-ratio:9/16;border-radius:4px;object-fit:cover;" controls playsinline><source src="your-reel-filename.mp4" type="video/mp4"></video>
Place the MP4 file in the same folder as this HTML file, and use its exact filename in src="...". For photos, replace any <div class="media-placeholder"> with <img src="your-photo.jpg" style="width:100%;border-radius:4px;">.
